valid_meta.principle: Hybrid runtime failures should be debugged as concrete ngUpgrade interop problems: inspect the error, remove stale AngularJS registrations that shadow downgraded components, and verify binding behavior with focused tests.

When a React 18 class component has multiple async setState calls but no post-await state-dependent decision, consolidate or sequence the updates without flushSync unless an intermediate render must be visible to the user.
When acting in an Expo UI Jetpack Compose codebase, use Compose-native components and assets: Host for the tree, LazyColumn for lists, and Android XML drawables for Icon.
During comment insertion, repair comments that violate the configured educational style by changing only comment text and then rechecking line count and syntax.
When editing investor updates, preserve a consistent cadence and metric table, state one headline, one win, one challenge, and make asks specific enough for investors to act on.
Troubleshoot and update Arize AI integrations by acting on the requested ax command first, then verifying with get/list output and preserving secrets through environment variables.
During comment insertion, repair comments that violate the configured educational style by changing only comment text and then rechecking line count and syntax.
Reduce client bundle size by reading import boundaries first, then replacing broad or heavy imports with direct and dynamic imports that preserve rendering behavior.
Existing prep files are user state: read before writing, preserve manual notes, and update only stale recommendations supported by current calendar organizer and timing fields.
During implementation, update track markers from concrete evidence: completed tasks need SHAs, active work uses [~], and metadata counts must match the plan.
Centralize repeated Tailwind visual decisions in semantic tokens and typed component variants rather than scattering raw utility colors across feature files.
When repairing an existing llms.txt, update or remove references based on actual filesystem evidence and keep the file curated rather than exhaustive.
When an MCP CLI call fails, repair from the tool schema and error text instead of repeatedly guessing JSON shapes.
Troubleshoot and update Arize AI integrations by acting on the requested ax command first, then verifying with get/list output and preserving secrets through environment variables.
When shell scripts process filesystem output, preserve structure with arrays or NUL-delimited reads; quote-only fixes are insufficient after word splitting already happened.
When a resource group is specified, move directly into read-only Azure discovery, collect detail records for relationship evidence, and verify every discovered resource appears in the diagram.
For LWC user-triggered mutations, use imperative Apex with validated public inputs and escaped Lightning UI rather than wired DML or raw DOM output.
Repair visual hierarchy by changing role-based typography, spacing, and icon sizes in the component, then verify responsive screenshots for overflow and density regressions.
When reviewing App Store findings, correct overclaims by re-checking cited files and downgrading unsupported blockers instead of preserving severe but false risks.
When users ask for a model comparison, stay inside the requested model set and decide from the prompt file's actual workload signals rather than generic capability rankings.
Assess relationships and schema from model metadata before changing cross-filter direction or cardinality.
When discovery is already complete, synthesize a focused llms.txt immediately from the evidence instead of rescanning or dumping the whole tree.
When Slack GIF output fails validation, repair the concrete mismatch shown by the validator rather than redesigning unrelated animation behavior.
When generating SDK snippets, encode return-value semantics in the code instead of hiding them in prose.
When GitLab image publishing fails, fix authentication, immutable tagging, and ref rules directly instead of broadening pipeline execution.
During integrity verification, react to each concrete mismatch as evidence and fix the verifier or source state without regenerating a manifest that might hide tampering.
When validation finds unsupported codebase-doc claims, repair by returning to source evidence, replacing guesses with [TODO] or [ASK USER], and rerunning the validation loop.
During comment insertion, repair comments that violate the configured educational style by changing only comment text and then rechecking line count and syntax.
When an artifact is complex but the draft is unbundled or visually generic, repair by returning to the generated project and bundle script.
When repairing .NET service code, remove sync-over-async and expected exception flow by propagating CancellationToken and returning typed Result failures.

When implementation drifts beyond the active plan step, compare the diff to the plan, remove or defer out-of-scope work, complete every item in the current step, and verify only with the command the plan names.
When a Playwright CLI action fails or behaves unexpectedly, refresh the snapshot and repair the action from current page state instead of retrying stale refs.
During plan execution, keep each task scoped to the reviewed plan, update progress mentally or explicitly, and let verification output decide whether to continue or stop.
Repair scaffolding by tracing concrete build errors back to framework, reference, or transaction-lifetime mismatches instead of broadening scope.
During refactor planning, tool feedback should correct the affected-file map and reorder phases when hidden dependencies appear.
During a dependency upgrade, let command output drive the next concrete migration step and keep the changed package set attributable.
When drafting the spike, keep the document actionable: use category-first kebab-case naming, concrete checklists, affected components, dependencies, and dated status history.
When reviewing or repairing a Copilot prompt, act from concrete mismatches between requirements, front matter, variable usage, and validation steps instead of broadly rewriting the prompt.
Preserve FlowStudio store metadata by reading existing tags before connector-risk classification updates.

After approval, cost work should become trackable execution: ensure labels, create one issue per optimization with evidence and commands, then create an EPIC that links the individual issues by priority.

When an existing Qdrant upgrade change violates compatibility rules, repair the concrete files by reducing the server jump to the next minor, aligning the SDK ahead of the server, and adding checks that prevent unsafe rollout.
When repairing a premium UI, act from measured rendering evidence: fix janky or inaccessible motion by changing the animation model and guards, then re-check the real page.
For recurring local schedules in DST zones, make invalid and ambiguous time policy explicit before changing code.
Before appending examples, compare proposed user fields to an exported schema and strip platform-managed fields so the dataset does not drift silently.
When a draft answers before required context is available, replace the diagnosis with the requested file checklist and explicitly preserve uncertainty.

When implementing ASP.NET Core containerization, perform the concrete edit loop in dependency order: copy project files for restore caching, publish in an SDK stage, configure runtime environment before entrypoint, and verify with docker build output.
During comment insertion, repair comments that violate the configured educational style by changing only comment text and then rechecking line count and syntax.
Prefer native form controls over custom ARIA when the interaction matches standard HTML semantics.
Prefer native form controls over custom ARIA when the interaction matches standard HTML semantics.
Migrate class components to hooks by mapping lifecycle semantics explicitly, preserving async cleanup and dependency behavior, then running focused tests.
Repair startup-model burn and runway logic from concrete formula outputs, preserving unrelated assumptions and validating sign conventions across scenarios.
For dynamic async resources, register each acquisition immediately with AsyncExitStack so later failures close earlier resources while preserving exception propagation.
Prefer native form controls over custom ARIA when the interaction matches standard HTML semantics.
When Power BI diagnostics point to relationship propagation, act on schema/filter design before rewriting unrelated DAX.

Troubleshoot CodeQL failures by reading the actual log symptom and changing the smallest workflow input that addresses that symptom, especially permissions, build mode, source root, and SARIF category.
When a bypass crashes, debug the side effects of the skipped branch and repair with the smallest evidence-backed edit instead of broad NOPs.
When replay or boundary failures point to non-deterministic workflow code, patch the workflow/activity split directly and verify the original replay command rather than masking the error.
For short Finnish text under 500 words, rewrite directly and return the naturalized text first with a compact pattern summary.
When remediating ASI tool and policy gaps, patch the enforcement path itself: fail closed before tool execution, validate names and arguments deterministically, and prove denied cases with tests.

Focused prompt repairs should act from concrete parser failures, making the smallest contract change that addresses malformed shape, missing keys, or hallucinated values, then rerunning the failing fixtures.
Prefer native form controls over custom ARIA when the interaction matches standard HTML semantics.
When repairing SwiftUI iOS UI, act on concrete HIG violations from code or build output: fixed frames, nonsemantic colors, missing labels, unsafe controls, and non-Hashable navigation values.
Remote cache configuration is a trust boundary: local builds should read shared cache without uploading, CI should opt into upload explicitly, and effective flags must be verified.
When Expo upgrade diagnostics fail, repair the concrete failing surface from the tool output and rerun the same harness instead of applying unrelated package churn.
For broad web QA, split ScoutQA into focused executions so authentication, core workflows, and accessibility each get specific prompts and their own execution metadata.
When asked for an Arize deep link, use the requested resource template exactly and stop to ask for browser-derived IDs when numeric path IDs would create a broken link.
When parallel workers collide on a shared file, preserve useful parallelism by assigning one owner to the shared file and converting other edits into explicit requests.
